description: Durable lead ledger: every core lead ever recorded, keyed by external_id. A scrape run consults this FIRST and skips leads already present, so money is not spent re-extracting known leads. Local mirror of the Supabase 'lead' table. evidence_quote is empty on legacy-migrated leads because the original run kept no verbatim quotes.

leads.summary: Leander ISD plans a summer 2026 replacement of the end-of-life artificial turf at AC Bible Jr. Memorial Stadium (football/soccer), funded by about $1.6M in reallocated 2023 bond savings.

evidence.project_name: Bible Stadium - turf replacement, football/soccer field
evidence.details: Leander ISD Bond Oversight Committee recommendation (approved by the Board on Jan. 22, 2026) to reallocate approximately $1.6 million in savings from the 2023 bond's high school baseball/softball turf projects to replace the end-of-life artificial turf playing surface at AC Bible Jr. Memorial Stadium, a football/soccer stadium used by multiple Leander ISD high schools. Replacement planned for summer 2026 to avoid disrupting athletic seasons; reported as underway as of May 2026 per the district's bond athletics projects page. Procurement method and contractor not yet identified in public sources; the district previously used Hellas Construction for related 2023 bond turf work.

leads.summary: Leander ISD plans a summer 2026 replacement of the end-of-life artificial turf at Ed W. Monroe Memorial Stadium (5,000-seat football/soccer stadium), funded by about $1.6M in reallocated 2023 bond savings.

evidence.project_name: Monroe Stadium - turf replacement, football/soccer field
evidence.details: Leander ISD Bond Oversight Committee recommendation (approved by the Board on Jan. 22, 2026) to reallocate approximately $1.6 million in savings from the 2023 bond's high school baseball/softball turf projects to replace the end-of-life artificial turf playing surface at Ed W. Monroe Memorial Stadium, a 5,000-seat football/soccer stadium (artificial surface with track and soccer capability) serving Vandegrift HS and other campuses. Replacement planned for summer 2026 to avoid disrupting athletic seasons; reported as underway as of May 2026 per the district's bond athletics projects page. Procurement method and contractor not yet identified in public sources; the district previously used Hellas Construction for related 2023 bond turf work.

leads.summary: Completed Leander ISD 2023 bond project: baseball/softball turf installed at five high schools, awarded to Hellas Construction for $27.6M; no further procurement expected.

evidence.project_name: LISD High Schools - baseball/softball turf installation
evidence.details: Bond 2023 project: installation of artificial turf on baseball and softball fields at five high schools (Cedar Park HS, Leander HS, Rouse HS, Vandegrift HS, Vista Ridge HS); Glenn HS already had turf prior to this bond. Awarded to Hellas Construction; cost $27,601,380; reported complete as of March 2025 (Vandegrift HS ribbon-cutting). Included for pipeline completeness as an awarded/recently-completed bond project; no further procurement action expected.

leads.summary: Completed Leander ISD 2023 bond project: artificial turf on the Running Brushy Middle School competition field ($1.19M, complete August 2025).

evidence.project_name: Running Brushy MS - competition field turf
evidence.project_address: 2303 N. Lakeline Blvd, Cedar Park, TX 78613
evidence.details: Bond 2023 project: installation of artificial turf on the competition field at Running Brushy MS. Cost $1,186,500; reported complete as of August 2025. Included for pipeline completeness as an awarded/completed bond project.

leads.summary: Round Rock ISD 2024 bond: replacement of the competition football/soccer turf at Chisholm Trail Middle School, one of a five-campus middle-school package; construction cited for fall 2026 (source dates conflict).

evidence.project_name: Chisholm Trail MS - competition football/soccer turf replacement
evidence.details: Round Rock ISD 2024 Bond Program ($932M, second tranche $300M): replacement of competition football/soccer field synthetic turf at Chisholm Trail Middle School, part of a five-campus middle school turf replacement package (Chisholm Trail, Hernandez, Hopewell, Pearson Ranch, Walsh). HPM is the bond program manager. Note: source dates conflict — the 2024 Bond Tracker cites construction beginning fall 2026 and completing late 2027; the Purchasing/e-bid portal cites beginning fall 2025 and completing late 2026. Projected_Start_Date__c below reflects the Bond Tracker figure pending confirmation.

leads.summary: Round Rock ISD active RFP (due 02/19/26): baseball/softball field turf and athletic improvements at Westwood High School, funded by the $932M 2024 bond.

evidence.project_name: Westwood HS - baseball/softball turf, athletic improvements
evidence.details: 2024 bond package ($932M) funds installation of artificial turf on the baseball and softball fields at Westwood High School, plus running track resurfacing and other athletics facility renovations. Listed on the district's active procurement board as 'Westwood HS Athletic Improvements - Phase 1' (RFP, due 02/19/26).

leads.summary: Round Rock ISD active bid (due 11/25/25): softball and baseball field renovation at Stony Point High School; turf scope referenced in the listing, specifics need validation.

evidence.project_name: Stony Point HS - softball/baseball field renovation Package 1
evidence.details: Active bid 'Stony Point HS - Package 1' (due 11/25/25) for renovation of softball and baseball fields and fencing, track surfacing renovation, and a new PEMB batting cage addition. Turf scope for the softball/baseball fields was referenced in the district's e-bid listing; sport/turf-material specifics require validation (needs_validation).
